# Pr 182345 Variance Aware Autoscaler Intermittent Surge Fairness

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_302783937bc849ecb7ef7323233d1efe
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
variance-aware-autoscaler-and-intermittent-surge-fairness

Add variance-aware autoscaler controller and per-tenant variance telemetry; improves stability under intermittent surge workloads and increases fairness of burst allocation.
Summary: Introduces a new variance-aware autoscaler that factors request-rate variance and burstiness into scale decisions and burst token allocation. This is targeted at intermittent-surge workloads (spiky, short-lived bursts from many tenants) where standard utilization-based autoscaling either overreacts or under-allocates burst capacity. Motivation: We observed several support incidents where tenants with highly variable arrival patterns caused oscillation in node counts and unfair consumption of burst tokens, degrading tail latency for other tenants. This change aims to reduce oscillation, make burst distribution fairer across tenants, and provide observability to tune policies. Key components: 1) controller/autoscaler/variance_controller.go: new controller that computes per-tenant mean and variance over a sliding window, derives an "effective demand" metric combining 95th percentile rate and variance penalty, and exposes decisions to the scaling plane; 2) pkg/scheduler/fair_burst_allocator.go: integrates variance metric into burst token allocation algorithm, preferring tenants with consistently high short-term variance to receive temporary soft credits while enforcing tenant-level fairness caps; 3) pkg/telemetry/metrics_exporter.go: new Prometheus metrics and Grafana dashboard templates for per-tenant request variance, effective_demand, burst_credit_usage, and burst_credit_hitrate; 4) cmd/controller-manager/main.go: wires the new controller behind a feature flag (--enable-variance-autoscaler); 5) helm/charts/autoscaler/templates/: adds config options for sliding window length, variance weight, and maximum soft burst per-tenant. Design notes: - Effective demand = alpha * p95_rate + beta * sqrt(variance), where alpha and beta are tunable. Default alpha=1.0, beta=0.5. - Hysteresis and cooldown: dynamic cooldown scales with variance to avoid thrash: cooldown = base_cooldown + gamma * normalized_variance. - Burst fairness uses a time-decayed ledger so transient spikes don't permanently drain credits. Tests and CI: unit tests added for variance estimator, allocator fairness invariants, and edge cases (zero-traffic, constant traffic, synthetic heavy-tail spikes). Integration test added in perf-canary suite simulating 1k tenants with exponentially distributed spike arrivals to validate stability and tail latency. CI: all checks passed after two iterations (lint, unit, integration perf-lite). Rollout plan: gated behind --enable-variance-autoscaler and feature gate variance_autoscaler_v1. Step 1: deploy to canary clusters with default beta=0.5 and monitor metrics for 48h. Step 2: if tail latency improves and no regressions, enable in staging clusters. Step 3: gradual rollout to Dedicated fleet with telemetry-backed rollback triggers (burst_credit_hitrate drop or node oscillation rate > threshold). Backwards compatibility: default behavior unchanged when feature flag disabled. Migration: none. Observability and dashboards: new Grafana panels added (per-tenant variance heatmap, effective_demand distribution, burst_credit_consumption by tenant percentile). Performance impact: controller runs O(N) over active tenants; optimized with shard workers and sampling for large clusters. Implementation notes and tradeoffs: - We intentionally use sqrt(variance) to keep units closer to rate while still reflecting variability. - To limit overhead, high-cardinality tenant metrics are aggregated into percentiles by default; full per-tenant metrics are optional via config. - There is a small potential for temporary unfairness during burst draining windows; the time-decayed ledger mitigates this. Links: Related Linear tasks ENG-3492 (variance estimator), ENG-3620 (fair allocator integration). Related PRs: none.
Aisha Rahman (comment): Can you clarify how we tune beta for different fleet sizes? Author reply: Default beta=0.5 is conservative; added docs section outlining tuning guidance based on observed p95/variance ratios and a small script to compute suggested beta from telemetry.
Diego Morales (requested changes): The allocator loop has a potential O(M*N) path when reconciling many tenants; please add sharding and a cap. Author reply: Reworked allocator to use sharded workers and bounded queue, updated tests and added benchmark results to PR description.
Ethan Liu (approval): Reviewed the integration tests and perf numbers. Approve after CI green.
